headline.purpose: Page titles only (Connections, a workflow's name).

label.purpose: Mono. Status pills, counts, timestamps, IDs - anything that's a measurement, not prose.

motion.name: ease-out-expo
motion.purpose: Default easing for all state transitions and reveals.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one primary action per screen (Sync, Connect, Log in, Generate summary).
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: oklch(0.62 0.18 48); color: #0a0a0a; font-family: var(--font-geist-mono); font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.02em; padding: 10px 18px; border: none; border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 120ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: oklch(0.68 0.18 48); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px oklch(0.09 0 0), 0 0 0 4px oklch(0.62 0.18 48 / 0.6); } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { background: oklch(0.30 0.004 48); color: oklch(0.62 0.006 48); cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary actions (Cancel, Log out, secondary nav links).
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { background: transparent; color: oklch(0.93 0.004 48); font-family: var(--font-geist-sans); font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; padding: 10px 18px; border: 1px solid oklch(0.26 0.004 48); border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 120ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { border-color: oklch(0.58 0.06 230); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px oklch(0.09 0 0), 0 0 0 4px oklch(0.62 0.18 48 / 0.6); }

components.name: Status Pill - Failing
components.description: Filled pill for the failing health state - the most saturated element in the system on purpose.
components.css: .ds-pill { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; font-family: var(--font-geist-mono); font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.02em; padding: 3px 10px; border-radius: 999px; } .ds-pill-failing { background: oklch(0.58 0.21 25); color: #0a0a0a; }

components.name: Status Pill - Silent
components.description: Filled pill for the silent health state.

components.name: Status Dot - Healthy
components.description: Healthy state never gets a filled pill - a small dot plus muted mono text keeps a healthy dashboard quiet (The Calm Default Rule).
components.css: .ds-status-dot { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; font-family: var(--font-geist-mono); font-size: 0.75rem; color: oklch(0.62 0.006 48); } .ds-dot { width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 50%; flex: none; } .ds-dot-healthy { background: oklch(0.68 0.13 152); }

components.name: Card / Panel
components.description: Workflow and connection cards. Hairline border + one surface step up from bg, never a shadow.
components.html:
Onboarding flow

Sends a welcome email and creates a CRM record when a new signup webhook fires.
components.css: .ds-card { background: oklch(0.155 0.004 48); border: 1px solid oklch(0.26 0.004 48); border-radius: 10px; padding: 24px; } .ds-card-title { font-family: var(--font-geist-sans); font-size: 1.0625rem; font-weight: 500; color: oklch(0.93 0.004 48); margin: 0 0 8px; } .ds-card-body { font-family: var(--font-geist-sans); font-size: 0.9375rem; line-height: 1.6; color: oklch(0.93 0.004 48); margin: 0; max-width: 70ch; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Form fields across login, signup, connect, and settings.
components.css: .ds-input { background: oklch(0.21 0.004 48); border: 1px solid oklch(0.26 0.004 48); border-radius: 6px; padding: 10px 12px; color: oklch(0.93 0.004 48); font-family: var(--font-geist-sans); font-size: 0.9375rem; width: 100%; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: oklch(0.62 0.006 48); } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: oklch(0.58 0.06 230); box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px oklch(0.09 0 0), 0 0 0 4px oklch(0.62 0.18 48 / 0.6); }

components.name: Top Navigation
components.description: Single top bar, replaces the current plain border-gray nav.
components.html: WatchdogConnections
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; gap: 24px; padding: 12px 24px; background: oklch(0.155 0.004 48); border-bottom: 1px solid oklch(0.26 0.004 48); } .ds-nav-brand { font-family: var(--font-geist-mono); font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.9375rem; color: oklch(0.62 0.18 48); text-decoration: none; } .ds-nav-link { font-family: var(--font-geist-sans); font-size: 0.875rem; color: oklch(0.62 0.006 48); text-decoration: none; } .ds-nav-link-active { color: oklch(0.58 0.06 230); }

narrative.overview: Watchdog is read like an oscilloscope, not browsed like a SaaS dashboard. The reference point is old monitoring instrumentation - amber phosphor displays, analog dials, control-room consoles - where every glowing element exists to report a fact, not to decorate. The product's own principle states it plainly: status is the headline, and a healthy system should look quiet. This system makes that literal: a near-black surface that disappears at rest, one amber signal color reserved for what actually needs attention, and typography split cleanly between human-readable prose (Geist Sans) and exact-readout data (Geist Mono) - counts, timestamps, IDs always render in mono, the way a terminal would print them. It explicitly rejects the generic SaaS-dashboard look this project's PRODUCT.md calls out by name: no purple/blue gradients, no identical icon-card grids, no hero-metric tiles with a gradient accent. There is one brand color, used sparingly, and a second cool accent for quieter UI; that's the entire decorative vocabulary.
keyCharacteristics: Dark by default - built for the moment someone opens this after an alert email, not a bright first-run demo.
One amber signal color, spent almost entirely on primary actions and the brand mark - never on status.
Status colors are a closed, separate palette from brand colors.
Mono type for anything that's data; sans for anything that's prose.
Flat by default - no drop shadows. Depth comes from three stepped surface lightness levels.

rules.name: The One Signal Rule
rules.body: Terminal Amber appears only on primary actions and the wordmark. If you reach for it anywhere in the status system, stop - that's instrument-blue or a status color's job, not the brand's.

rules.name: The Calm Default Rule
rules.body: Only failing and silent render as filled, saturated pills. healthy renders as quiet text with a small solid dot; unused and orphaned-at-rest render as plain secondary-text. A fully healthy dashboard should look almost monochrome.

rules.name: The Readout Rule
rules.body: Any number that describes a measurement of the system (run counts, error counts, timestamps, durations) renders in Geist Mono at the Label size, even mid-sentence. Anything a human wrote renders in Geist Sans.

rules.name: The Bezel, Not Glow Rule
rules.body: Cards and modals separate from their background with a 1px hairline border and a lighter surface step - never a drop shadow. The one exception is the focus ring.

dos: Do keep Terminal Amber to primary actions and the wordmark only.
Do render healthy and unused as quiet, low-color text states.
Do set every number that measures the system in Geist Mono.
Do separate every surface with a hairline border and a lighter surface step, never a shadow.
Do pair every status color with a text label, never color alone.
donts: Don't use purple/blue gradients or a gradient-accented hero-metric tile.
Don't build identical icon+heading+text card grids as a default layout move.
Don't use border-left/border-right as a colored accent stripe on any card, list row, or alert.
Don't use background-clip: text gradient headings.
Don't add a drop shadow anywhere outside the one allowed focus-ring glow.
Don't let a healthy dashboard look as colorful as a broken one.
